summary: eslint-helper executes obfuscated shell payloads via base64-decoded child_process invocations
details: The package's main entry (index.js) uses Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString() to decode hidden payloads and passes them to execSync via 'bash' and 'zsh' shells. The combination of base64-decoded payload material (index.js line 9, with additional decoders at lines 194-195) feeding into shell execSync calls (lines 101, 117) is the canonical obfuscated-shell-dropper / RCE shape, executed when the module is loaded. The package name 'eslint-helper' suggests positioning as a developer tooling utility, increasing the likelihood of being required in build/lint contexts where the obfuscated shell commands will run. There is no legitimate reason for an eslint-related helper to decode base64 blobs into shell strings and execute them.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
